task_completion.description: Load the Cholera Toxin hexamer structure (PDB: 1XTC) into the molecular viewer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The PyMOL viewer displays a protein structure, and the object panel or command history shows '1XTC' has been fetched or loaded.

task_completion.description: Visually separate the catalytic A-subunit from the B-pentamer ring.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The A-subunit (central/top chain) is visually distinct from the ring of 5 B-subunits, either through distinct coloring, different representations (e.g., cartoon vs surface), or spatial separation in the viewer.

task_completion.description: Highlight the primary receptor-binding residue, Tryptophan 88 (Trp88), on all B-subunits.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Specific residues on the outer edges of the B-pentamer ring are highlighted, typically shown in 'sticks' or 'spheres' representation and possibly colored differently from the rest of the chain.

task_completion.description: Identify B-pentamer residues within 4.0 Angstroms of the A-subunit's anchoring helix.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The PyMOL command line, object panel, or viewer shows a selection or distance measurement (e.g., dashed lines) representing contacts between the central anchoring helix and the surrounding pentamer ring.

task_completion.description: Render and save a publication-quality figure of the complex to ~/PyMOL_Data/images/cholera_ab5.png.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal or file explorer shows the file 'cholera_ab5.png' created in the '/home/ga/PyMOL_Data/images/' directory, or the PyMOL command line shows a successful 'png' save command to this path.

task_completion.description: Write a structural report to ~/PyMOL_Data/cholera_interface_report.txt containing chain IDs, Trp88 count, and interface residues.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output (e.g., cat command) displays the contents of 'cholera_interface_report.txt' in '/home/ga/PyMOL_Data/', showing a list of chains, a count of Trp88 residues, and a list of Chain:Resi pairs.

integrity.description: The agent actually used PyMOL to perform the structural analysis and rendering.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the PyMOL GUI open with the structure loaded and manipulated, or terminal history showing interactive PyMOL commands, rather than just writing a script without executing it.

integrity.description: The agent derived the report data from actual structural queries rather than hallucinating or hardcoding fake answers.
integrity.visual_evidence: The PyMOL command history or script output shows the agent actually running selection or distance commands (e.g., 'select', 'distance', 'iterate') to find the interface residues before writing them to the text file.

integrity.description: The rendered image corresponds to the visual state constructed by the agent in PyMOL.
integrity.visual_evidence: The final visual state of the protein in the PyMOL viewer matches the expected output of the saved 'cholera_ab5.png' file, rather than copying an external image.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Note a severe factual contradiction in the prompt: The prompt claims the B-pentamer is 'chains B-F' and the anchoring helix is 'chain A residues 195-240'. In reality, PDB 1XTC's pentamer is chains D, E, F, G, H (there is no chain B), and chain A 195-240 are water molecules (the actual helix is chain C 196-240). The agent may struggle with this. If the agent uses the correct chains (D-H and C) or attempts to follow the prompt but gets stuck on missing chains, evaluate based on their logical PyMOL steps and visual progress. The final figure must be at /home/ga/PyMOL_Data/images/cholera_ab5.png and the report at /home/ga/PyMOL_Data/cholera_interface_report.txt.
